Introduce a new Parameter Object and name it forgery:
content:
The `forgery` parameter object is a record in Java that does not have any fields or methods. It is used to represent an empty or placeholder for a parameter that will be passed to a method or function. In this case, the `R` record is used as a placeholder for the `i` parameter in the constructor of the `R` class.

The `@SuppressWarnings("preview")` annotation is used to suppress the "preview" warning from the Java compiler. This warning is typically issued when using new features or language constructs that are not yet fully supported by the Java platform. In this case, the `record` keyword was introduced in Java 16 as a preview feature, so the compiler will issue a warning if it detects any use of the `record` keyword.

Overall, the `forgery` parameter object is a simple and effective way to represent an empty or placeholder for a parameter that will be passed to a method or function.
